---
title: "Overview"
format: 
  html:
    code-fold: true
---

## Goals

Accelerate the use of uncrewed vessels (UxS) to decrease dependence on large scale ship surveys. PAM-Gliders provide an opportunity to assess populations of marine mammals (protected species) and the environment to augment existing shipboard surveys.

## Objectives

Field testing of PAM-glider platforms to provide data-driven guidance on the capabilities of each system to (1) work in different operational environments and (2) address different research questions. This 2-week sea trial was conducted off Oahu, Hawai’i in February 2026.

## Approach

Collect comparable data (at the same time and place) during the 2-week sea trial to assess several glider capabilities. Gliders transited tracklines with a 'Figure 8' design, while conducting several mission modes, with each mode serving as an in-situ experiment for assessing piloting strategies and impacts on navigation, endurance, and noise. Modes include:

-   Optimize to survey coverage

-   Optimize for low noise

-   Intermediate approach to survey coverage and noise reduction

-   Drifting (Quiet Mode)

1.  **Piloting:** To achieve mission goals, different gliders may have different approaches, advantages, and limitations. These approaches may have differing impacts on other critical components (navigation, endurance, noise). Also, each glider platform has a unique approach to piloting requiring different levels of expertise and abilities to modify a mission.

2.  **Navigation:** Different platforms vary in their ability to overcome challenges due to currents and navigating hazards. This may affect their suitability to work in certain environmental conditions, the survey area they can cover, flight efficiency and endurance, and the additional noise resulting from maintaining course (which reduces quality of acoustic data).

3.  **Endurance:** Primary endurance concerns are related to battery endurance (glider platform, environmental sensors, and PAM sensors) and PAM data storage. Survey modes provide a means of assessing power and storage endurance ranges under a variety of mission conditions to inform future survey planning.

4.  **Noise:** Our ability to use PAM gliders to effectively study specific species, anthropogenic noise, and/or the marine soundscape is necessarily dependent on interference by self-noise produced by the glider platform and its components. For each glider, we will study the source of each noise identified and the degree to which these noise sources interfere with study objectives.

The Glider Rodeo will include glider teams from Pacific Islands Fisheries Science Center, Southwest Fisheries Science Center as well as academic partners (Oregon State University, Cornell University) and industry partners (Alseamar, Teledyne, and Jasco). For more information, see links to [Glider Platforms](https://nmfs-ost.github.io/PAM-Glider/content/gliders.html) and [PAM Sensors](https://nmfs-ost.github.io/PAM-Glider/content/sensors.html).

The Glider Rodeo is part of a larger effort to develop a Transition to Operations plan for PAM-Gliders. For more information on these larger efforts, visit our [PAM-Glider site](https://nmfs-ost.github.io/PAM-Glider/).

![Glider Rodeo draft figure-8 tracklines planned for offshore Hawaii.](images/RodeoTracks.png){width="511"}

```{r setup}
#| include: false
source(here::here("_code/_common.R"))
here()

# ============================================
# CONFIGURATION - EDIT THESE VALUES
# ============================================
SURVEY_PASSWORD <- "rodeo26"  # Change this to your desired password
DIAGRAM_FILE <- "GliderRodeo_flowchart-Overview.drawio.svg"   # Your SVG diagram filename
```

## Data management Plan

Flowchart identifying different components of the Data management Plan, including Data Collection (fieldwork), Hardware, Pre-Processing of data, Data Analysis, and Reporting.

::: callout-note
Right-Click on flowchart components to open link in new tab to learn more about each component.
:::

```{=html}

<!-- Diagram Controls -->
<div class="svg-controls">
  <button onclick="zoomIn()">🔍 Zoom In</button>
  <button onclick="zoomOut()">🔍 Zoom Out</button>
  <button onclick="resetView()">⟲ Reset View</button>
  <button onclick="togglePanMode()" id="pan-toggle">🔒 Lock Pan</button>
  <span id="zoom-level">Zoom: 100%</span>
</div>

<!-- SVG Viewer -->
<div id="svg-viewer" class="svg-viewer-container"></div>



<script>
  // ============================================
  // CONFIGURATION
  // ============================================
  const SURVEY_PASSWORD = '`r SURVEY_PASSWORD`';
  const DIAGRAM_PATH = '../output/`r DIAGRAM_FILE`';


  // ============================================
  // SVG DIAGRAM WITH PAN/ZOOM
  // ============================================
  (function() {
    var container = document.getElementById('svg-viewer');
    var scale = 1;
    var translateX = 0;
    var translateY = 0;
    var isDragging = false;
    var panEnabled = true;
    var startX, startY;
    var svg;
    
    fetch(DIAGRAM_PATH)
      .then(response => response.text())
      .then(svgText => {
        container.innerHTML = svgText;
        svg = container.querySelector('svg');
        
        if (svg) {
          svg.removeAttribute('width');
          svg.removeAttribute('height');
          
          var viewBox = svg.getAttribute('viewBox');
          if (!viewBox) {
            var bbox = svg.getBBox();
            svg.setAttribute('viewBox', `${bbox.x} ${bbox.y} ${bbox.width} ${bbox.height}`);
          }
          
 
// More aggressive link interception
var links = svg.querySelectorAll('a');
console.log("Found", links.length, "links in SVG");

links.forEach(function(link) {
  var href = link.getAttribute('href') || link.getAttribute('xlink:href');
  console.log("Link href:", href);
  
link.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
  e.preventDefault();
  e.stopPropagation();
  
  var href = this.getAttribute('href') || this.getAttribute('xlink:href');
  console.log("Clicked link with href:", href);
  
  // Strip diagrams.net URL if present
  if (href && href.includes('#survey:')) {
    var surveyPart = href.substring(href.indexOf('#survey:'));
    console.log("Opening survey for:", surveyPart);
    var parts = surveyPart.replace('#survey:', '').split('|');
    var shapeId = parts[0];
    var shapeText = parts[1] || shapeId;
    openSurvey(shapeId, shapeText);
  } else if (href) {
      console.log("Opening survey for:", href);
      var parts = href.replace('#survey:', '').split('|');
      var shapeId = parts[0];
      var shapeText = parts[1] || shapeId;
      openSurvey(shapeId, shapeText);
    } else if (href) {
      console.log("Opening external link:", href);
      window.open(href, '_blank');
    }
  });
});
          updateTransform();
        }
      })
      .catch(error => {
        container.innerHTML = '<p style="color: red; padding: 20px;">Error loading diagram: ' + error.message + '</p>';
      });
    
    container.addEventListener('mousedown', function(e) {
      if (e.target.tagName === 'A' || e.target.closest('a')) {
        return;
      }
      
      if (panEnabled) {
        isDragging = true;
        startX = e.clientX - translateX;
        startY = e.clientY - translateY;
        e.preventDefault();
      }
    });
    
    document.addEventListener('mousemove', function(e) {
      if (isDragging && panEnabled) {
        translateX = e.clientX - startX;
        translateY = e.clientY - startY;
        updateTransform();
      }
    });
    
    document.addEventListener('mouseup', function(e) {
      isDragging = false;
    });
    
    container.addEventListener('wheel', function(e) {
      e.preventDefault();
      var delta = e.deltaY > 0 ? 0.9 : 1.1;
      
      var rect = container.getBoundingClientRect();
      var mouseX = e.clientX - rect.left;
      var mouseY = e.clientY - rect.top;
      
      translateX = mouseX - (mouseX - translateX) * delta;
      translateY = mouseY - (mouseY - translateY) * delta;
      
      scale *= delta;
      scale = Math.max(0.1, Math.min(scale, 10));
      
      updateTransform();
    });
    
    function updateTransform() {
      if (svg) {
        svg.style.transform = `translate(${translateX}px, ${translateY}px) scale(${scale})`;
        svg.style.transformOrigin = '0 0';
        document.getElementById('zoom-level').textContent = 'Zoom: ' + Math.round(scale * 100) + '%';
      }
    }
    
    window.zoomIn = function() {
      scale *= 1.2;
      scale = Math.min(scale, 10);
      updateTransform();
    };
    
    window.zoomOut = function() {
      scale *= 0.8;
      scale = Math.max(scale, 0.1);
      updateTransform();
    };
    
    window.resetView = function() {
      scale = 1;
      translateX = 0;
      translateY = 0;
      updateTransform();
    };
    
    window.togglePanMode = function() {
      panEnabled = !panEnabled;
      var btn = document.getElementById('pan-toggle');
      if (panEnabled) {
        btn.textContent = '🔒 Lock Pan';
        container.style.cursor = 'grab';
      } else {
        btn.textContent = '🔓 Enable Pan';
        container.style.cursor = 'default';
      }
    };
  })();
  
  updateResponseCount();
</script>
```
